title: Helix CLI: Ticket Lookup, Help, JSON Output & Inspection Ergonomics
description: This tour walks through a set of fixes addressing five defect categories in the Helix CLI: (1) HTTP 404s caused by raw ticket ID passthrough to API endpoints that require internal CUIDs, (2) missing --help interception at global, router, and command levels, (3) absence of --json output mode for agent/script consumption, (4) 'Invalid Date' rendering from unsafe Date construction, and (5) PowerShell quoting problems in inspect db. The fix follows a bottom-up dependency chain: shared utilities first, then the core resolver module, then global help, then router refactoring, then individual command fixes, and finally tests and build verification.

steps.description: Root cause RC-2: no subcommand checked for --help/-h before dispatching, so help flags either errored or executed command logic. This file introduces isHelpRequested(args) and shared helpers hasFlag, getFlag, and getPositionalArgs. All help checks across the codebase depend on isHelpRequested existing here first — it is the foundation of the entire help-before-work principle. Data flow: every command router and leaf command calls isHelpRequested(args) at the very top of its handler before any config loading or API calls.
steps.title: Step 1 — Shared flag utilities and isHelpRequested

steps.description: Root cause RC-1: resolveTicketId() in tickets/index.ts returned raw user input unchanged, so short IDs like 'BLD-339' and numeric strings like '339' were interpolated directly into API paths expecting internal CUID-format IDs, yielding HTTP 404. This new module implements matchTicket() with three-tier priority matching against the /api/tickets list: (1) exact internal ID match, (2) case-insensitive shortId match, (3) numeric suffix extraction from shortId. resolveTicket() fetches the list endpoint, calls matchTicket(), and on ambiguity or no-match throws explicit errors that include the org name for context. The pattern mirrors the proven resolve-repo.ts fetch-list-then-match approach already in the codebase.
steps.title: Step 2 — Core shared ticket resolver (replaces raw passthrough)

steps.description: Acceptance criterion AC-10 requires 30 unit tests covering all resolution scenarios without requiring live API access. Tests cover matchTicket with internal ID, shortId, numeric reference, ambiguous matches, empty lists, and priority ordering; plus extractTicketRef, isHelpRequested, hasFlag, getFlag, and getPositionalArgs. Uses node:test (built-in Node >=18, zero new dependencies). These tests can be run via the test script added to package.json and serve as a regression guard for the resolver logic.
steps.title: Step 3 — Unit tests for resolver and flag utilities

steps.description: Without a test script entry, 'npm test' was not wired up. This change adds the test script pointing at the node:test runner so the 30 unit tests are executable via standard npm workflows and CI quality gates. No new runtime dependencies are introduced.
steps.title: Step 4 — Add test script to package.json

steps.description: Root cause RC-2 (top-level): the main switch in src/index.ts had no --help/-h check before dispatching, meaning 'hlx --help' would fall through to error or attempt config loading. This change adds a configOrHelp() guard at the global entry point: if --help or -h is present in process.argv, usage text is printed to stdout and the process exits cleanly without any API calls or config requirements. This satisfies AC-7's requirement that help flags work at the global level without authentication.
steps.title: Step 5 — Global --help/-h interception before config loading

steps.description: This is the central refactoring step. The old resolveTicketId() (lines 13-26) returned raw input unchanged — replaced entirely by extractTicketRef() + resolveTicket() from resolve-ticket.ts. All 6 ticket subcommands (get, artifacts, artifact, rerun, continue, bundle) now receive a resolved internal CUID, eliminating the source of 404s. Lines 35-37 add router-level help check so 'hlx tickets --help' prints usage without dispatching. Lines 57-59 add per-command help passthrough so each subcommand can also intercept --help before doing any work. Data flows: user input → extractTicketRef → resolveTicket (fetches list, matches) → internal CUID → subcommand.
steps.title: Step 6 — Ticket router: adopt shared resolver, add router-level help

steps.description: Root cause RC-3: new Date(rawString) was called without validation, rendering 'Invalid Date' for malformed or missing timestamps. Lines 32-44 add a formatDate() helper that validates with isNaN() and cross-references runStatus to decide whether to display 'in progress', 'unknown', or the formatted date string. Root cause RC-4: no machine-readable output existed. Lines 85-94 check --json flag and output the full ticket object as JSON.stringify with untruncated description, satisfying AC-6. The existing text output path is unchanged, preserving backward compatibility.
steps.title: Step 7 — formatDate fix (Invalid Date) and --json output mode

steps.description: Acceptance criterion AC-5: 'hlx tickets list --json' must output all TicketItem fields including the internal id field so agents and scripts can capture CUIDs for subsequent commands. Lines 90-93 add JSON.stringify(items) output when --json is present. The text output path is also updated to include the internal ID column, which was previously omitted, making it easier for humans to copy-paste IDs for other commands.
steps.title: Step 8 — tickets list: --json output and internal ID in text output

steps.description: Root cause RC-2 (command level): latest.ts executed its full logic even when --help was passed. This change adds isHelpRequested(args) at the top of the handler, printing usage and returning early without fetching the list endpoint or calling printTicketDetail. This is the same pattern applied uniformly across all ticket subcommands.
steps.title: Step 9 — latest.ts: help check before command logic

steps.description: create.ts previously ran validation logic (checking for required flags) before checking --help, meaning 'hlx tickets create --help' would print a validation error instead of usage text. Adding isHelpRequested before validation ensures help is always available without side effects, consistent with the help-before-work principle.
steps.title: Step 10 — create.ts: help check before validation

steps.description: continue.ts (lines 12-15) compared positional[0] === ticketId to filter the ticket reference from the continuation context arguments. After resolution, ticketId is the internal CUID while positional[0] is still the raw user input (e.g., '339'). Without this fix, the raw ticket reference would be forwarded as a context argument to the rerun. The fix passes the raw reference as a fourth parameter (rawRef) so the positional filter compares against the original input, not the resolved CUID. Help check is also added at the top.
steps.title: Step 11 — continue.ts: rawRef parameter for positional arg filtering

steps.description: src/comments/index.ts contained a duplicate resolveTicketId() (lines 6-14) with identical raw-passthrough logic — a copy-paste of the defect in tickets/index.ts. This change removes the duplicate entirely and adopts extractTicketRef + resolveTicket from resolve-ticket.ts, ensuring comments commands (which also accept ticket references) benefit from the same three-tier matching and error handling. Help handling is also added consistent with other routers.
steps.title: Step 12 — comments router: remove duplicate resolver, adopt shared resolver

steps.description: Root cause RC-5: 'hlx inspect db' accepted SQL only as positional arguments, which broke on PowerShell when SQL contained Postgres double-quoted identifiers because PowerShell strips double quotes. Lines 57-59 add a --query (-q) flag as a safe alternative input path: users can pass SQL as a flag value, which PowerShell handles correctly. Help checks are added for all inspect subcommands. PowerShell-safe example strings are added to the usage text (AC-11). The positional arg path is preserved for backward compatibility.
steps.title: Step 13 — inspect db: --query flag and PowerShell-safe examples

steps.description: Acceptance criterion AC-9: unresolved or ambiguous references must fail with clear errors including org context so users know which workspace was searched. Lines 117-119 produce an ambiguous match error listing all matching ticket IDs and the org name. Lines 123-126 produce a not-found error including the input ref and org name. With these errors in place, no command silently falls through or returns a confusing 404 — all failure modes are explicit, actionable, and include enough context for the user to diagnose the problem. All major code paths (resolution, help, JSON output, timestamp formatting, PowerShell quoting) are now covered by the implementation and verified by the 30 unit tests.
steps.title: Step 14 — Error messages with org context (summary and clean paths)
